page: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 8 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core workloads. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 10% tier. Conversely, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, consistently rank in the middle 50% average tier. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, along with Passmark memory scores, also align with average tier expectations. Notably, the CPU encryption test under Passmark registers a strong performance in the top 25% tier, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5a.12xlarge utilizes older-generation AMD EPYC hardware to deliver a high density of dedicated vCPUs and memory resources at a lower qualitative cost profile compared to newer-generation general-purpose instances. This resource density makes it suitable for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core clock speeds. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and redis-based caching, where multi-core scaling and memory capacity are prioritized over single-threaded execution speed.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance tradeoff: single-core performance, including Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, ranks in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average middle 50% tier. Notably, CPU encryption performance is strong, placing in the top 25%. Given its high resource density and qualitative cost-efficiency profile, the m5a.12xlarge is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that leverage its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio without requiring high single-core execution speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, offering balanced multi-core performance and strong encryption capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is a general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high memory capacity for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM.
Delivers strong cryptographic performance, ranking in the top 25% for CPU encryption.
Features a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Exhibits average multi-core performance alongside lower single-core execution speeds.
Suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and database operations.
